### **Job Title**

Internship: Data Strategy
### **Job Description**

**Start Date: As of September 2026**

**Duration: at least / 6 months**

**Type: Internship (non\-thesis)**

**Weekly Presence: 40h (minimum 32h)**

**Location: Amsterdam**

**Internship Title**

**Data Analyst Intern – Marketing Analytics**

**Your role:**

We’re looking for a curious and driven Data Analyst Intern to support our Marketing Analytics team. In this role, you will help analyze campaign performance, customer behavior, or funnel management data to generate insights that guide marketing decisions. You’ll work with datasets, build dashboards, and contribute to insights that supports stakeholders across marketing and commercial teams.


**Key Responsibilities**

* Collect, clean, and analyze marketing and customer data.
* Build and maintain dashboards and reports (e.g., in Power BI/Qlik).
* Support performance analysis and experiment measurement.
* Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in data.
* Collaborate with marketing and data engineering teams on ad\-hoc insights.

**You’re the right fit if:**

* Currently pursuing a bachelor’s/master’s degree in data, analytics, business, or a related field.
* Strong analytical mindset with basic knowledge of SQL, Python, or Excel.
* Interest in AI (any experience strongly preferable), marketing, analytics and data storytelling.
* Ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights.

**How we work together**

We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office\-based teams, this means working in\-person at least 3 days per week. Onsite roles require full\-time presence in the company’s facilities. Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.


**We offer you:**  

A non\-stop growth opportunity within a global multinational company. In addition, we also want to renumerate you well for the opportunity. This includes:


* Monthly full\-time allowance between €500 \- €700 gross, depending on your education level and if you need to relocate to The Netherlands. Part\-time internships receive the allowance pro\-rata.
* Housing compensation. Should you need to relocate to near our office, we’ll offer you a monthly allowance of €300,\- net if location is in the vicinity of Amsterdam, €255,\- net for all other Philips locations. To be eligible, we’ll just ask for the rental contract from your landlord, and your usual home\-work travel distance needs to be more than 50 km or 1\.5 hours of travel time (for a one way trip).
* Travel compensation, if you are not eligible for a free public transport card; you will receive max. €192,\- net as a monthly allowance.
* Paid holidays per internship term.
* The opportunity to buy Philips products at our Philips shop.

We are looking for an enthusiastic individual who can join our Strategic Intelligence Data Science team within the Strategy \& Corporate Development department, for a minimum of 6 months from August/September 2026 onwards.


**Who we are**


Atradius provides trade credit insurance, surety and collections services worldwide through a strategic presence in 50\+ countries. Its products help protect companies throughout the world from payment risks associated with selling products and services on trade credit.


The Strategy \& Corporate Development (SCD) department supports the Management Board directly through market and portfolio insights, strategic initiatives, and product and service design. The Strategic Intelligence team within SCD is responsible for providing analytical insights as part of our strategy formulation and implementation.


**What will you work on?**


The successful candidate will be part of Strategic Intelligence’s Data Science sub\-team, contributing to the team's analytical activities and supporting the delivery of data\-driven insights to senior stakeholders.


Here are some examples of tasks and responsibilities:


* Contributing to the monthly reporting tasks of the team.
* Maintaining and improving our team’s SQL Server database.
* Automating our reporting processes using SQL and R.
* Analysing and visualising data.
* Undertaking your own analyses to bring fresh insights/ideas to the team.

**Who are we looking for?**


* Holds a BSc or MSc in a quantitative field (e.g. statistics, life/physical sciences, econometrics, data science).
* Fluent in English.
* Strong SQL skills are required. Knowledge of T\-SQL and Microsoft SQL Server would be an advantage.
* Experience with Power BI dashboards is desirable.
* Knowledge of R or another programming language would be a bonus.
* Eligible to live and work in the Netherlands.
* Able to work 60% of the time in the Amsterdam office.

**A great applicant should:**


* Have an inquisitive mindset.
* Be committed to finding better ways to do things.
* Work through problems by themselves, but is also not afraid to ask questions when stuck.
* Have an interest in data, automation, and data visualisation.
* Have solid organisation and communication skills.

* A friendly and challenging environment to learn: international team (25 colleagues with 10 different nationalities spread over 6 European cities).
* Professional and engaging culture.
* The opportunity and guidance for you to improve your skills and experience.

description:
Climate risk is becoming increasingly relevant for the planning, design and operation of data centres. Data centres play an important role in the digital economy, but their design standards are often still based on historical weather data. These standards do not always account for future changes in physical climate hazards, such as extreme heat, flooding, drought, water scarcity, storms or other climate\-related stressors.  

At Haskoning, we see climate resilience for data centres as a growing market. We want to strengthen our understanding of the physical climate risks affecting data centres worldwide and further develop our proposition in this field.  

Haskoning has strong expertise in data centre design, climate hazard datasets and physical climate risk assessments. This internship brings these capabilities together.  

During this graduate internship, you will study global physical climate risks for data centres. You will combine technical climate risk analysis with practical engineering knowledge and support the development of Haskoning’s commercial proposition for climate\-resilient data centres. Your work may also contribute to a white paper on this topic.

**What you do as an Intern**
----------------------------


We are looking for a motivated MSc student who is interested in physical climate risks, vulnerability analysis and practical application in an engineering and consultancy environment.
During the internship, you will work across teams and combine climate data, GIS analysis, engineering insights and market\-oriented thinking. You will help assess how different climate hazards may affect data centres in different regions and what this means for design, resilience and adaptation.
The student will work on or support the following activities:
* Using GIS workflows to perform physical climate risk assessments for data centre locations.
* Applying our in\-house Twinn climate hazard datasets and Multi\-Hazard Risk Tool to analyse risks to current and future climate hazards.

* Supporting the development of a Haskoning white paper on global climate risks for data centres.
* Supporting Haskoning’s commercial proposition for climate risk analysis and climate resilience services in the datacentre sector.
* Contributing to a small market study on the potential demand for climate resilience services in this sector.

Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

description:
About Cordys Analytics
Cordys Analytics is a fast\-growing medtech start\-up and spin\-out from UMC Utrecht.


We develop GALVO, a full\-spectrum AI\-powered cardiology platform that helps healthcare professionals deliver faster, more accurate and more scalable cardiac diagnostics. GALVO supports multiple diagnostic workflows, including resting ECG analysis, Holter monitoring and future applications in remote cardiac monitoring and wearable devices.


Our mission is to improve cardiovascular care by combining artificial intelligence with clinical expertise, enabling earlier diagnosis, better patient outcomes and more efficient healthcare delivery.


As our Holter Operations continue to grow rapidly across Europe, we are looking for an analytical and hands\-on intern or graduate student to help us further professionalise, optimise and scale this critical part of our business.


The assignment
Holter Operations form one of the core services delivered through the GALVO platform in Europe. Holter recordings are received, analysed by trained ECG analysts supported by AI, reviewed through quality control, and returned to healthcare providers as high\-quality clinical reports.


As volumes continue to grow, we want to manage this operation in an increasingly systematic and data\-driven way. To support this, we have developed a Holter Operations Management System: a practical scorecard and capacity management framework that enables us to manage demand, capacity, quality, productivity and financial performance on a weekly basis.


As an intern, you will help further develop, validate and expand this management system, both qualitatively and quantitatively. Rather than writing a report that ends up on a shelf, you will contribute to a management tool that is actively used during our weekly Holter Operations meetings and directly supports operational decision\-making.


Research question
The main research question for this internship or graduation project is:

*How can Cordys Analytics design, measure and manage its Holter Operations so that capacity, quality, turnaround time and profitability remain under control as volumes grow rapidly?*


Possible sub\-questions include:

* How can forecast Holter demand be translated into the required analysis and QC capacity?
* Which KPIs are needed to manage capacity, quality, productivity and financial performance on a weekly basis?
* How can analyst recruitment, onboarding and training be incorporated into capacity planning?
* Which operational buffers are required to prevent backlog growth and turnaround time deterioration?
* How can a practical scorecard be designed that genuinely supports weekly operational decision\-making?

What you will do
You will contribute to a practical management system for a rapidly growing healthcare operation. Typical activities include:

* Mapping the end\-to\-end Holter Operations process, from intake and analysis to QC, reporting and customer delivery.

description:
**About this position**
Autonomous Transport: a vision for the distant future, or closer than you think? During this internship, you will explore the societal and system\-wide impact of large\-scale deployment of Connected Automated Transport (CAT) on a busy international corridor linking two major European ports. By contributing to this research, you will help shape a more innovative and future\-ready logistics system.


**What will be your role?**
During this internship, you will provide insight into the system\- and societal impact of CAT on the Rotterdam\-Antwerpen corridor. You will analyse success factors and bottlenecks of border crossing CAT and you’ll develop a model to quantify the effects on congestion, emissions and labour demand. You also examine the extent to which the effects are desired or undesired, and what the course of action is to prevent or, conversely, stimulate effects.


During the internship, you are part of a project team specialized in the integration of autonomous transport in logistical operations. You will have the opportunities to share and discuss your ideas within this team.


**What we expect from you**
For this internship (\+/\- 6 months), we are looking for a student with a technical background and affinity with logistics, automotive, vehicle technology or traffic engineering. You have basic experience with python, or are able to get up to speed quickly. You are socially engaged, results driven and able to think outside the box. You combine these qualities with a strong dose of enthusiasm to work on this logistical analysis from both technical and societal perspective. During this internship you are part of the project team and enjoy to work with others and you will learn to work with a lot of autonomy and ownership over your own work. description:
We offer you an Ocean of Possibilities . Join our family.


**About us**

Damen aims to become the world's most sustainable and digitally connected shipyard. The Research, Development \& Innovation (RD\&I) department develops and implements the technology and know\-how to achieve these ambitions. We actively assist the business in creating an innovative product portfolio and provide forward\-thinking guidance to improve the quality and performance of Damen's products and services. You will be joining the Data Science team within Damen RD\&I located in Gorinchem. Our department focuses on applying cutting\-edge data and AI solutions to Damen's shipbuilding and maritime operations. The team includes domain experts in physics\-informed machine learning, simulation acceleration, predictive maintenance, computer vision, and operational analytics. This internship is part of Smart Maintenance, a strategic project aimed at using AI to detect abnormal equipment behavior on board vessels before it leads to failure or unplanned downtime.  



**The role**

As an intern, you will work on our Smart Maintenance project where we have developed a n anomaly detection algorithm, which aims to help engineers spot early signs of equipment problems, such as engines, pumps, propulsion and cooling systems, before they escalate into failures. Vessels generate huge amounts of sensor data during operation, and our goal is to turn that data into reliable, trustworthy signals that support maintenance decisions. You will contribute to an existing pipeline that learns what "healthy" equipment behavior looks like and flags deviations from it. Your primary focus will be on a dedicated research topic, to be selected together with the team, that strengthens a specific part of this pipeline — from data selection to detection reliability, health trending, explainability, or deployment. There is room to shape the exact topic based on your interests and background, either before or shortly after you start. This can be a thesis/graduate internship and could start from September onwards.


Possible research topics that we offer, on which the final scope is to be defined together:


* *Model transferability across vessels:* exploring how an anomaly detection model trained on one vessel can be adapted to other vessels, machinery types, or operating environments — including retraining, recalibration, and drift detection strategies.
* *Reliable anomaly detection:* improving detection models to minimize false alarms, adapt to different operating conditions, handle transient events, and quantify prediction confidence.
* *Health and degradation trending:* moving beyond fault detection to identify gradual performance degradation, developing health indicators that give early warning of wear or efficiency loss.
* *Explainable AI and fault diagnosis:* making anomaly models explainable, identifying which sensors or components drive an alert, and supporting root\-cause analysis for engineers.

description:
**About this position**
Would you like to work with unique equipment and enjoy a different challenge every day? Then the Measurement Technology team of Reliable Structures at the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO) is the place for you!


The Measurement Technology team instruments unique test setups in the mechanical testing hall of the Delft Construction Innovation Lab and far beyond. In the testing hall, for example, offshore LNG hoses are aged, sheet pile wall thicknesses are measured, and the tensile strength of the latest wind turbine blades is determined. In addition to work in the hall, you will regularly go into the field to perform on\-site measurements, such as measuring the prestressing force in the bolts of one of the Dutch storm surge barriers, the Balgstuw at Ramspol, or installing monitoring systems on major steel bridges in the Netherlands and in the tallest buildings of the Benelux region. All of this is done so that scientists can support their recommendations with the measurement data you help collect.


**What will be your role?**
During your internship, you will assist in preparing, setting up, and carrying out these measurements. Equipment maintenance and calibration may also be part of your responsibilities. The work involves a wide range of activities, including designing measurement setups, connecting equipment, programming, and analyzing data. There will be very little sitting still. There is also room for you to develop expertise in new measurement techniques such as Digital Image Correlation (DIC) and Acoustic Emission (AE), as well as the control and operation of fixed tensile testing machines. You will work alongside experienced measurement technicians who can teach you the practical skills of the profession. Depending on your educational background and interests, we can offer a suitable set of tasks that will allow you to further develop your skills.


At the Delft Construction Innovation Lab, you will work alongside colleagues from the Reliable Structures (RS) department. RS consists of approximately 50 people with various technical backgrounds. In the lab, we work as a team of seven colleagues, including three measurement technicians. You will therefore be part of a small and diverse group. During your internship at the DBL lab, you will work closely with your supervisor. Through regular progress meetings, we ensure that you are comfortable in your role and able to make progress on your assignment. Each day starts with a joint meeting with your supervisor. This is when the supervisor outlines the day's schedule. You will also have the opportunity to share your own plans and ideas for the day, after which you will create a schedule together.

Financial Markets (FM) is the Bank’s gateway to the professional markets around the world. Our primary function is to service the needs of ING’s clients with products, services and guidance. FM aims to be a significant player in our market leader and challenger markets, with an additional presence in all the major international markets and specialist expertise in selected emerging markets and products.


**Your team**


The FM Quant team is a global team within the FM organization that provides quantitative expertise required to price and risk manage derivatives products. The main tasks of the team are in the area of model analytics and development.


The team based in Amsterdam consists of about 15 professionals that develop pricing and risk models that are used globally by different trading locations and risk departments, with a special emphasis on xVA, (counterparty credit) and market risk models. We are closely interacting with IT DevOps engineers In integrating to libraries into Front and Back\-Office systems.


**Your activities**


You work under the guidance of experienced team members on a topic at the interface of academia and industry with a focus on computational and/or mathematical methods for pricing and risk modelling of derivatives portfolios. The exact project will depend on the profile of the candidate.

A stimulating environment with a strong team consisting of quantitative analysts and developers who are working closely on the development of pricing and risk infrastructure in close collaboration with users from trading, sales and risk management departments.

**Dit ga je doen**
------------------

The Netherlands Enterprise Agency (Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland; also known by its Dutch acronym RVO) is looking for a motivated intern for the period October 2026 to June 2027 (9 months preferred, 6\-8 months negotiable) to support the Orange Corners Research and Data team.


The Orange Corners team is in need of an enthusiastic research intern, with a strong background in qualitative research methods and experience with data analysis (i.e. literature review, coding, interviews, processing and analyzing datasets). As a research intern, you will engage in advancing our ongoing research projects based on the Orange Corners Research Agenda , as well as support the continuous, data\-driven development of the Orange Corners programme. You will be part of an ambitious team, where you will be offered a dynamic task package with a lot of room for your own interpretation. As an Orange Corners research intern, you will switch between gathering data (e.g. literature reviews, interviews and surveys) analyzing and processing data (e.g. transcribing, coding or analyzing in excel) as well as contributing to high quality research.


For instance, you will assist in academic research on the effects of shocks (i.e. armed conflict, or hyperinflation) on entrepreneurship support in fragile states or on the difference in training needs of entrepreneurs who experience linear vs. exponential growth. Another example is analyzing drivers and barriers for Orange Corners entrepreneurs to formally register their business. You will liaise with researchers within Orange Corners and at universities both within and outside of the Netherlands where the research projects are taking place. You will also be in contact with implementers, private partners and knowledge institutions that have a vital role in our research.


You will also play an role in the monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) of the Orange Corners programme. Through our MEL framework, Orange Corners collects and analyzes data from participants, hubs, and ecosystem actors, enabling the enhancement of programs. Your role in this field is diverse. For example, you will be in regular contact with programme implementors and colleagues to ensure programme results are submitted, and you will actively be involved in analysing and presenting these results to the wider team, using various platforms to communicate the programme’s impact, such as visual dashboards (POWER BI).

HEINEKEN Nederland zoekt joú!  

Bij HEINEKEN kom je terecht in de wereld van bier, vakmanschap en horeca. Een omgeving vol ambacht, lekkere producten en veel gezelligheid. Waar het draait om het verbinden van mensen, want dat is al ruim 150 jaar ons vak. Diversiteit is een van de pilaren die ons sterk maakt. We hebben hierin nog stappen te zetten, maar leren iedere dag. Kan jij ons hierin helpen en durf jij ons een spiegel voor te houden?



De stage  

Als stagiair(e) binnen het Channel Execution team zal je ondersteuning bieden aan Channel Specialist Sports aan het National Accounts team. Zo ben je onderdeel van het bredere marketingteam dat verantwoordelijk is voor HEINEKEN Nederland in de Horeca. Je focus zal liggen op evenementen en met name de Heineken Business Club \& Sport. Ook ben je verantwoordelijk voor initaiteven in samenwerking met de KNVB en de introductie van Heineken 0\.0 Ultimate.



Binnen deze stage ben je verantwoordelijk voor activaties in het horecakanaal met en voor onze grootste klanten. Denk aan grote horecaketens, pretparken, hotels en bioscopen. Bij deze klanten werken we aan tailormade campagnes en evenementen. Je zal in samenwerking met de Channel Specialist een rol spelen in het organiseren van deze campagnes en evenementen. Je hebt veel klantcontact om samen met onze horecaklanten succesvolle activaties neer te zetten. Er is veel ruimte voor jouw creativiteit en ondernemerschap.



Ook zal je ondersteuning geven voor onze online horeca shop waar klanten en account managers promotie materiaal kunnen bestellen voor hun zaak.



Tijdens je stage krijg je de ruimte om HEINEKEN als bedrijf te leren kennen, ook buiten jouw afdeling en HEINEKEN. We geven jou het vertrouwen en de vrijheid, zodat je als een echte ondernemer te werk kunt gaan. We halen de buitenwereld graag naar binnen en daarom omarmen wij nieuwe, frisse ideeën met enthousiasme. Uiteraard altijd met de begeleiding die voor jou passend is.



De afdeling  

De stageplek is onderdeel van het Channel Execution Team. Het team dat zich bezighoudt met het winnen binnen Out of Home (horeca)! Dit team bestaat uit 10 personen en werkt dagelijks aan de kanaal strategie en aan activaties van onze merken binnen de Nederlandse horeca. Tijdens je stage heb je contact met veel verschillende afdelingen waardoor je HEINEKEN Nederland goed leert kennen. Wel zo handig voor na je studie. Werken in de horeca tak van HEINEKEN betekent ook dat je tijdens je stage veel borrels en feestjes in de horeca zult hebben.
